primary_methods: cell type annotation via manual gating
differential cell type composition (linear mixed effects)

analytical_questions.primary_question: Can the simpleSeg and CellProfiler pipelines be reliably joined on object ID so that validated per-cell intensities and spatial coordinates can be combined for spatial analysis of CAF2 fibroblasts in HCC?
other_questions: Is the per-core file structure, ROI metadata, and object ID mapping consistent across segmentation masks, intensity files, and clinical metadata?
Do simpleSeg and CellProfiler produce consistent per-cell marker intensities?
What explains the aSMA discrepancy between the two pipelines?
Is there evidence of over-segmentation in the provided segmentation masks?
Are the FlowJo manual-gating cell type annotations biologically coherent?
Do cell type proportions differ between tumour and non-tumour tissue?

embedding.source_text: Spatial Validation of CAF2 Fibroblasts in Hepatocellular Carcinoma Using Imaging Mass Cytometry | Elijah Willie Elijah Willie Jie Kang Ellis Patrick Jean Yang Angela Ferguson | Can the simpleSeg and CellProfiler pipelines be reliably joined on object ID so that validated per-cell intensities and spatial coordinates can be combined for spatial analysis of CAF2 fibroblasts in HCC? | Is the per-core file structure, ROI metadata, and object ID mapping consistent across segmentation masks, intensity files, and clinical metadata? Do simpleSeg and CellProfiler produce consistent per-cell marker intensities? What explains the aSMA discrepancy between the two pipelines? Is there evidence of over-segmentation in the provided segmentation masks? Are the FlowJo manual-gating cell type annotations biologically coherent? Do cell type proportions differ between tumour and non-tumour tissue? | Are all images, masks, and clinical metadata complete and correctly structured? file audit; 91 images, 89 masks, 88 shared ROIs; image/mask alignment and metadata checks Are the segmentation masks correctly registered to the raw images? plotPixels mask overlays on DNA1/CD45/CK8 for representative ROIs Do simpleSeg and CellProfiler measure the same cells with consistent intensities? per-marker Pearson correlation (>0.9999 for all markers except aSMA, r = 0.083) Is there evidence of over-segmentation in the masks? per-ROI cell area distribution vs 50 µm² threshold; cell count vs median area scatter Are marker distributions consistent across ROIs after normalisation? per-ROI density plots before/after trim99+mean+PC1 normalisation Are the cell type annotations biologically coherent? grouped marker expression heatmap, UMAP, and per-ROI cell type composition | per-cell segmentation quantification marker intensity normalisation cross-pipeline intensity validation over-segmentation assessment cell type annotation via manual gating UMAP dimensionality reduction differential cell type composition (linear mixed effects) spatial co-localisation analysis spatial neighbourhood/niche identification | imaging mass cytometry spatial proteomics multiplexed tissue imaging | hepatocellular carcinoma | CAF2 fibroblasts cancer-associated fibroblasts tumour microenvironment cell segmentation cell type composition over-segmentation aSMA PACE framework | cytomapper simpleSeg EBImage spicyR lisaClust scater lme4 lmerTest

sample_info.sample_type: HCC liver tissue microarray cores (tumour and non-tumour ROIs across two TMAs, HCC3 and HCC4); ~1.27 million segmented cells over 88 shared ROIs

qc.qc_question: Are all images, masks, and clinical metadata complete and correctly structured?
qc.qc_method: file audit; 91 images, 89 masks, 88 shared ROIs; image/mask alignment and metadata checks

qc.qc_question: Are the segmentation masks correctly registered to the raw images?
qc.qc_method: plotPixels mask overlays on DNA1/CD45/CK8 for representative ROIs

qc.qc_question: Do simpleSeg and CellProfiler measure the same cells with consistent intensities?
qc.qc_method: per-marker Pearson correlation (>0.9999 for all markers except aSMA, r = 0.083)

qc.qc_question: Is there evidence of over-segmentation in the masks?
qc.qc_method: per-ROI cell area distribution vs 50 µm² threshold; cell count vs median area scatter

qc.qc_question: Are marker distributions consistent across ROIs after normalisation?
qc.qc_method: per-ROI density plots before/after trim99+mean+PC1 normalisation

qc.qc_question: Are the cell type annotations biologically coherent?
qc.qc_method: grouped marker expression heatmap, UMAP, and per-ROI cell type composition

title: Spatial Validation of CAF2 Fibroblasts in Hepatocellular Carcinoma Using Imaging Mass Cytometry

primary_methods: pseudobulk aggregation by sample and cell type

analytical_questions.primary_question: Does ketogenic diet modify chemotherapy-associated transcriptional responses in leukaemia cell subtypes?
other_questions: Are low-quality cells, slide-localisation artefacts, and batch effects sufficiently controlled before downstream analysis?
Can the Xenium single-cell profiles be assigned to biologically interpretable leukaemia-like and normal PBMC cell types?
Which genes and pathways change between baseline and chemotherapy within each cancer-like cell type?
At the chemotherapy timepoint, which genes and pathways differ between ketogenic and control diet groups?
Does diet alter the magnitude or direction of chemotherapy-associated gene-expression changes in specific leukaemia cell types?
Are leukemic stem-cell-like and blast-like populations distinguishable for cell-type-specific downstream analysis?

sample_info.sample_type: dissociated PBMC cells from human leukaemia patient samples

qc.qc_question: Are cells outside the expected slide/sample regions removed?
qc.qc_method: slide-coordinate filtering using sample boundary plots

qc.qc_question: Are low-quality cells removed using defensible thresholds?
qc.qc_method: genes detected >= 10, total transcripts >= 15, and cell area 20-300

qc.qc_question: Does the retained dataset remain large enough for downstream analysis?
qc.qc_method: 2,047,464 cells and 5,101 genes retained after QC

qc.qc_question: Are batch or sample effects visible after QC?
qc.qc_method: UMAP by batch and sample showed no obvious batch effect

qc.qc_question: Are unassigned cells lower quality and therefore suitable for removal?
qc.qc_method: genes detected, transcript count, and cell-area distributions; 13% unassigned cells removed

qc.qc_question: Are cell-type annotations biologically plausible?
qc.qc_method: scClassify with Van Galen AML reference, UMAP separation, and marker-gene validation

title: Xenium single-cell transcriptomic analysis of ketogenic diet and chemotherapy response in leukaemia

analytical_questions.primary_question: Are in-house USYD CUT&RUN peak calls of sufficient quality for downstream epigenomic analyses?
other_questions: Are technical duplicate CUT&RUN samples reproducible at the promoter-associated gene level?
How similar are in-house K562 CUT&RUN signals to Henikoff reference data?
Does pAG-MNase freeze-thaw cycling reduce CUT&RUN data quality?
What T-cell titration provides sufficient signal for sequencing and downstream analysis?
How strongly does antigen presentation alter promoter-associated CUT&RUN signatures?

qc.qc_question: Do the in-house datasets show adequate promoter-associated signal?
qc.qc_method: non-zero genes, zero genes, and library size summaries

qc.qc_question: Are technical duplicate samples reproducible?
qc.qc_method: Jaccard index and Fisher's exact test

qc.qc_question: Is the in-house K562 signal consistent with external Henikoff reference data?
qc.qc_method: promoter-associated gene overlap, Jaccard index, Fisher's exact test, and Venn diagrams

qc.qc_question: Does pAG-MNase freeze-thaw cycling affect data quality?
qc.qc_method: pairwise overlap across 1x, 2x, 4x, and 6x freeze-thaw conditions

qc.qc_question: Does T-cell titration affect peak detection?
qc.qc_method: pairwise overlap across 50k, 100k, 250k, and 500k cell inputs

qc.qc_question: Does antigen presentation induce expected biological variability in the signatures?
qc.qc_method: pairwise overlap and Venn diagrams across antigen presentation conditions

title: CUT&RUN protocol quality assessment for in-house pAG-MNase peak calls

primary_methods: manual hazard-ratio calculation from fixed-time survival proportions
indirect hazard-ratio derivation from shared reference comparisons

analytical_questions.primary_question: Are perineural invasion and its pathological features associated with worse patient outcomes across the cancer pathology literature?
other_questions: Is binary PNI presence versus absence associated with poorer survival outcomes?
Is multifocal PNI associated with poorer survival outcomes than unifocal PNI?
Is larger involved nerve size associated with poorer survival outcomes?
Is extratumoural or peritumoural PNI associated with poorer survival outcomes than intratumoural PNI?
Is the distance between tumour cells and nerves prognostic in resection specimens?
Can heterogeneous survival endpoints and effect measures be harmonised into analyzable endpoint groups for meta-analysis?

embedding.source_text: Meta-analysis of perineural invasion features and patient outcomes in cancer pathology literature | Yue Cao Jean Yang Dr Ruta Gupta | Are perineural invasion and its pathological features associated with worse patient outcomes across the cancer pathology literature? | Is binary PNI presence versus absence associated with poorer survival outcomes? Is multifocal PNI associated with poorer survival outcomes than unifocal PNI? Is larger involved nerve size associated with poorer survival outcomes? Is extratumoural or peritumoural PNI associated with poorer survival outcomes than intratumoural PNI? Is the distance between tumour cells and nerves prognostic in resection specimens? Can heterogeneous survival endpoints and effect measures be harmonised into analyzable endpoint groups for meta-analysis? | Are source papers classified correctly for meta-analysis eligibility? paper-level screening of 58 papers into binary PNI comparison, other comparisons, and non-useful papers Are extracted outcomes consistent with existing review data? sanity check of LLM-extracted outcomes against review spreadsheet for OS and DSS across 32 matched titles Are effect sizes sufficiently complete for quantitative synthesis? automatic and manual extraction-status assessment; 21 automatically extracted papers and 14 potentially manually extractable papers Are heterogeneous endpoints harmonised before meta-analysis? primary and secondary endpoint grouping strategy suggested by Professor Hubert Low Is one suitable analysis selected per paper and endpoint? prefer multivariate estimates when available, otherwise use univariate estimates Are subgroup comparisons eligible and interpretable? comparison-specific inclusion/exclusion rules and indirect HR derivation when direct comparisons were unavailable | systematic-review data curation LLM-assisted outcome extraction review endpoint harmonisation hazard-ratio extraction manual hazard-ratio calculation from fixed-time survival proportions indirect hazard-ratio derivation from shared reference comparisons random-effects meta-analysis multivariate-prioritised model selection forest plot visualisation heterogeneity assessment using I-squared | systematic review literature extracted clinical outcome data hazard ratios and confidence intervals survival outcomes radiotherapy outcomes | cancer | perineural invasion PNI survival outcomes hazard ratio focality nerve size PNI location tumour-nerve distance | readxl meta

qc.qc_question: Are source papers classified correctly for meta-analysis eligibility?
qc.qc_method: paper-level screening of 58 papers into binary PNI comparison, other comparisons, and non-useful papers

qc.qc_question: Are extracted outcomes consistent with existing review data?
qc.qc_method: sanity check of LLM-extracted outcomes against review spreadsheet for OS and DSS across 32 matched titles

qc.qc_question: Are effect sizes sufficiently complete for quantitative synthesis?
qc.qc_method: automatic and manual extraction-status assessment; 21 automatically extracted papers and 14 potentially manually extractable papers

qc.qc_question: Are heterogeneous endpoints harmonised before meta-analysis?
qc.qc_method: primary and secondary endpoint grouping strategy suggested by Professor Hubert Low

qc.qc_question: Is one suitable analysis selected per paper and endpoint?
qc.qc_method: prefer multivariate estimates when available, otherwise use univariate estimates

qc.qc_question: Are subgroup comparisons eligible and interpretable?
qc.qc_method: comparison-specific inclusion/exclusion rules and indirect HR derivation when direct comparisons were unavailable

title: Meta-analysis of perineural invasion features and patient outcomes in cancer pathology literature

analytical_questions.primary_question: How does ACOD1 expression and detection vary across public cardiac single-cell datasets, cell types, and heart failure disease groups?
other_questions: Do CAD and ACOD1 show different expression patterns in public cardiac single-cell datasets?
Is the proportion of ACOD1-expressing cells different between healthy and heart failure samples?
Is ACOD1 expression distributed differently across cardiac cell types?

qc.qc_method: expression > 0

qc.qc_method: minimum cells and donors per group

qc.qc_method: 4/191795 Kuppe cells and 508/881081 Reichart cells detected

analytical_questions.primary_question: What proteomic changes does the topical PDE4 inhibitor crisaborole induce in treated atopic dermatitis lesions, and how do they compare with the corticosteroid betamethasone?
other_questions: Which proteins change in treated AD lesions (DAY45 vs DAY1) under crisaborole, and under betamethasone?
Does the crisaborole versus betamethasone treatment effect differ in the treated-AD proteome (interaction contrast)?
Which biological pathways are altered by each treatment, and which are shared versus treatment-specific?
Are the differential-abundance results robust to outlier sample exclusion (CRIS05-DAY45-TAD, BETA16-DAY45-TAD), prior systemic treatment, and MNAR-aware modelling?
Do the headline findings align with published drug-mechanism and atopic dermatitis literature?

investigators.collaborator: Dr Ali Azimi and Professor Pablo Fernández-Peñas

sample_info.sample_type: tape-strip skin biopsies (treated AD, untreated AD, normal skin) across baseline and post-treatment timepoints from 15 patients

qc.qc_question: Is the cohort balanced and complete across the design?
qc.qc_method: per-patient completeness checks; 15 patients x 6 samples (90 analysis + 16 pooled QC injections)

qc.qc_question: Does missingness indicate sample- or feature-level quality concerns?
qc.qc_method: MNAR diagnostic (per-protein mean log2 vs % missing); per-sample missingness 10-77%, median ~45%, tissue-driven MNAR

qc.qc_question: Are sample intensity distributions aligned across the run?
qc.qc_method: per-sample log2 intensity boxplots/densities; Spectronaut normalisation judged sufficient, no extra global normalisation

qc.qc_question: Is instrument quantification reproducible and is there run-order drift?
qc.qc_method: pooled-QC %CV (median 17.3%) and median log2 vs injection index; small significant drift not structured in analysis samples

qc.qc_question: Which samples are global outliers?
qc.qc_method: PCA on complete-case proteins; CRIS05-DAY45-TAD and BETA16-DAY45-TAD flagged on PC2 (>70% missing / right-shifted intensity)

qc.qc_question: Should KNN imputation be the primary modelling input?
qc.qc_method: KNN vs NA-coded PCA/UMAP sensitivity; NA-coded matrix retained (KNN does not rescue outliers and adds a UMAP artefact)

title: Proteomic investigation of the effect of PDE4 inhibitor Crisaborole on atopic dermatitis

analytical_questions.primary_question: How many true eating disorder cases, and what total enrolment, are required to detect a clinically meaningful drop in screening tool sensitivity from 0.90 to 0.80 with at least 80% power?
other_questions: How does the assumed eating disorder prevalence translate the required number of true cases into total participant enrolment?
Do Monte Carlo simulations that treat the number of true cases as a Binomial(N, prevalence) draw confirm the analytical sample size?

Which sample size framework (precision-based Buderer's formula, binomial simulation, McNemar's paired test, exact binomial power, or correlation CI width) is most appropriate for the validation design?

embedding.source_text: Sample Size Determination for Eating Disorder Screening Tool Validation | Elijah Willie Elijah Willie Alistair Senior Dr Eve House | How many true eating disorder cases, and what total enrolment, are required to detect a clinically meaningful drop in screening tool sensitivity from 0.90 to 0.80 with at least 80% power? | How does the assumed eating disorder prevalence translate the required number of true cases into total participant enrolment? Do Monte Carlo simulations that treat the number of true cases as a Binomial(N, prevalence) draw confirm the analytical sample size? Which sample size framework (precision-based Buderer's formula, binomial simulation, McNemar's paired test, exact binomial power, or correlation CI width) is most appropriate for the validation design? | Where do the assumed screening tool sensitivity and specificity values come from? published estimates for IOI-S, SDE, ESP, and EDE-Q (Bryant 2021, Maguen 2018, Cotton 2003, Fairburn & Beglin 2008) Is the assumed eating disorder prevalence defensible? adopted conservative national 12-month ED prevalence of 4.45% (current rather than lifetime) Does the analytical power calculation agree with a stochastic data-generating process? Monte Carlo simulation (10,000 reps) drawing true cases from Binomial(N, 0.0445) How sensitive is the required sample size to the assumed prevalence and true sensitivity? power and total-enrolment curves across prevalence and true-sensitivity grids Is the adopted test appropriate relative to alternative designs? comparison of five approaches (Buderer's formula, binomial simulation, McNemar's test, exact binomial power, correlation CI width) | sample size determination statistical power analysis one-sample exact binomial test Monte Carlo power simulation diagnostic accuracy precision sizing (Buderer's formula) McNemar's paired test Fisher z correlation CI width | eating disorders | sample size statistical power screening tool validation sensitivity exact binomial test prevalence Monte Carlo simulation

sample_info.sample_type: people living with obesity (prospective recruitment for screening tool validation)

qc.qc_question: Where do the assumed screening tool sensitivity and specificity values come from?
qc.qc_method: published estimates for IOI-S, SDE, ESP, and EDE-Q (Bryant 2021, Maguen 2018, Cotton 2003, Fairburn & Beglin 2008)

qc.qc_question: Is the assumed eating disorder prevalence defensible?
qc.qc_method: adopted conservative national 12-month ED prevalence of 4.45% (current rather than lifetime)

qc.qc_question: Does the analytical power calculation agree with a stochastic data-generating process?
qc.qc_method: Monte Carlo simulation (10,000 reps) drawing true cases from Binomial(N, 0.0445)

qc.qc_question: How sensitive is the required sample size to the assumed prevalence and true sensitivity?
qc.qc_method: power and total-enrolment curves across prevalence and true-sensitivity grids

qc.qc_question: Is the adopted test appropriate relative to alternative designs?
qc.qc_method: comparison of five approaches (Buderer's formula, binomial simulation, McNemar's test, exact binomial power, correlation CI width)

title: Sample Size Determination for Eating Disorder Screening Tool Validation

primary_methods: gene-level collapsing of promoter-associated peak counts
limma-voom modelling with timepoint as predictor

sample_info.sample_type: CD8 and CD4 CAR T-cell chromatin profiling samples across serial stimulation timepoints

qc.qc_question: Are the input peak-by-sample data structures suitable for downstream analysis?
qc.qc_method: peak-by-sample matrix dimension check and duplicated-peak check

qc.qc_question: Are promoter-associated peaks defined consistently before gene-level analysis?
qc.qc_method: ChIPseeker annotation using TSS ±5 kb promoter regions

qc.qc_question: Are multiple peaks mapping to the same gene handled consistently?
qc.qc_method: sum promoter peak counts by annotated gene

qc.qc_question: Are comparisons restricted to the intended mark, cell type, and stimulation timepoints?
qc.qc_method: metadata filtering by histone mark, CD4/CD8 cell type, and 0x/3x/6x/9x timepoint

qc.qc_question: Are donor-sensitive CD8 comparisons checked for robustness?
qc.qc_method: sensitivity analysis excluding donor sRCHDO2

qc.qc_question: Are significance and effect-size thresholds documented for interpretation?
qc.qc_method: volcano plots using adjusted p-value, raw p-value, and absolute log2 fold-change cutoff of 1.5

title: Promoter-associated histone-mark differential enrichment across serially stimulated CAR T-cells

analytical_questions.primary_question: Can self-supervised wearable sensor representations support activity classification across wrist and thigh datasets?

Can Sydney wrist and thigh classifiers identify sitting, standing, walking, cycling, and related activities?
How well do classifiers transfer between Capture24 and Sydney wearable datasets?
Which normalisation strategy improves cross-dataset wearable activity classification?

qc.qc_question: Are wearable datasets harmonised to a common format?

qc.qc_method: Sydney, Capture24, and subject-wise normalisation compared

qc.qc_question: Does the model generalise across datasets and sensor placements?
qc.qc_method: Capture24-to-Sydney wrist/thigh transfer evaluation

analytical_questions.primary_question: Which transcriptional changes induced by NR treatment are mediated by SARM1 activation?
other_questions: Which genes and exons differ across NR, SARM1 inhibitor, combined treatment, and control conditions?
Are there genes with significant NR-by-SARM1 interaction effects?
Which genes show a suppression-like pattern where NR effects are reduced by SARM1 inhibition?
Which pathways, networks, and response-pattern clusters explain the SARM1-dependent effects?

sample_info.sample_type: bulk RNA-seq samples across four treatment conditions

qc.qc_question: Are raw sequencing data represented consistently across samples?
qc.qc_method: 12 samples; 4 FASTQ files per sample

qc.qc_question: Are reads assigned to genes at acceptable rates?
qc.qc_method: >80% featureCounts gene assignment

qc.qc_question: Are low-expression genes filtered before normalisation?
qc.qc_method: edgeR filterByExpr; 17089 of 78334 genes retained

qc.qc_question: Does normalisation address library-composition differences?
qc.qc_method: TMM-normalised logCPM

qc.qc_question: Are samples consistent after filtering and normalisation?
qc.qc_method: PCA, sample distance, correlation, mean-variance trend

qc.qc_question: Are count matrices aligned with sample metadata?
qc.qc_method: sample-name consistency checks for gene, exon, and DEXSeq inputs

title: Identify transcriptional changes induced by NR treatment and determine the interaction effect of NR treatment and SARM1 activation

analytical_questions.primary_question: Do Prkn x Polg double-mutant mice show accelerated transcriptional aging signatures compared with wild-type mice?
other_questions: Does the partial common aging signature differ between mt and wt mice at the cell-type level?
Does the partial common aging signature show spatial differences between mt and wt mice?
Are normal aging signatures enriched among mt versus wt differential expression results?
How much of the reference aging signature is represented in the study gene panel?

qc.qc_question: Are samples grouped consistently as mt and wt?
qc.qc_method: sample metadata from wt1-3 and mt1-3

qc.qc_question: Are expression values normalised before scoring?
qc.qc_method: logNormCounts(size.factor = cell_area)

qc.qc_question: Are low-information genes removed before VISION?
qc.qc_method: genes with zero counts removed

qc.qc_question: Is aging-signature feature coverage sufficient?
qc.qc_method: approximately 40% CAS gene-panel coverage

qc.qc_question: Is statistical power sufficient for formal testing?
qc.qc_method: three mice per genotype; descriptive trends noted

title: Transcriptional age acceleration analysis in the Prkn x Polg double mutator mouse model
